Japanese: 同日　午後１２時１３分 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: April 11, 12:13 PM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: それでは、審理を再開しましょう。 亜内検事、証人を呼んでください。
English: Court will now reconvene. Mr. Payne, please call your witness.

Japanese: それでは、現場で 事件の瞬間を目撃した証人‥‥
English: This next person is someone who witnessed the crime as it happened.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさんを 入廷させてください！
English: The prosecution calls Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne to the stand!

Japanese: （な、なによ、この ミョーなチンモクは‥‥）
English: (Wh-What's with this stiff silence...?)

Japanese: ‥‥長い裁判人生、さまざまな 証人にダマされてきました。
English: In my long career as a judge, I have been deceived by many witnesses.

Japanese: もう二度と、ダレも信用しない。 ‥‥そう決めていたのですが‥‥
English: It's my job to doubt; to take no one at their word...

Japanese: この証人のコトバなら！ 私、なんでも信じられそうです。
English: But in your case, I must admit that you radiate a glow of complete sincerity.

Japanese: （バカなコト言わないでよ！）
English: (I can't believe he actually said that!)

Japanese: あ。では証人。 名前をおねがいできますかな？
English: Oh! Um, now then, witness. Could you please state your full name?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥あ、あの。わたし‥‥
English: I, umm...

Japanese: ああ、大丈夫ですよ。 おびえる必要はありません！
English: Don't worry, sweetie. There's no need to be nervous!

Japanese: シツレイなことを言うヤツには、 私がカツンと言ってやりますぞ！
English: If anyone says anything rude, you can be sure, I'll cut them right down to size!

Japanese: 私も、この木槌でカツンと！
English: And I will bash them with my gavel!

Japanese: （ふたりとも、まっすぐ私を 見つめて言い切ったわ‥‥）
English: (I love how they look straight at me when they say that...)

Japanese: あの‥‥わたし、とても ほっとしておりますの。
English: Um, thank you... for calming my nerves.

Japanese: みなさん‥‥とっても親切で、 あたたかな方ばかりなんですもの。
English: You are all so nice... I almost feel right at home.

Japanese: いやいや！
English: Not at all!

Japanese: なんのなんの！
English: It was nothing!

Japanese: それで‥‥ケッキョク、 名前と職業は？
English: If we may move on now... What is your full name and occupation?

Japanese: ‥‥お名前は、美柳　ちなみと 申しますわ。
English: My name is Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: 勇盟大学の、文学部３回生ですの。
English: I'm a junior in Literature at Ivy University.

Japanese: ふつつか者ですが‥‥なにとぞ、 よろしくおねがいいたしますね。
English: I just want to say... It's an honor for me to be here in your noble presence.

Japanese: こちらこそ！
English: The honor is all mine!

Japanese: よろしくおねがいしますぞ！
English: No... The honor is all mine.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ね）
English: (Well, we know whose milkshake brings all the boys to the yard...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの。おじさまがた？
English: ...Umm. Sir?

Japanese: む。オジサマのことですかな？
English: Is there something I can help you with?

Japanese: このオジサマに、なんでも 言ってごらんなさい！
English: You just go on and say whatever is on your mind!

Japanese: きっと‥‥どこかに、大きな マチガイがあるのだと思いますわ。
English: I'm sure that there must be some kind of mistake.

Japanese: リュウちゃん、ひとごろしなんて ‥‥いたしませんもの。
English: Feenie wouldn't kill anyone. I just know it!

Japanese: うむうむ。 そうでしょうそうでしょう。
English: Yes, yes... I can see why you'd say that.

Japanese: （ホント、やっかいな証人ね‥‥）
English: (She's going to be a tough witness, alright...)

Japanese: （オジサンたちを、たった１２秒で 味方につけちゃったわ）
English: (It only took her 12 seconds to wrap them all around her little finger.)

Japanese: それでは、証言を おねがいしましょう。
English: Now then, please proceed with your testimony.

Japanese: 事件当日、おじょうさんが 目撃したことを。
English: Let's hear about what you witnessed on the day of the incident, if you please.

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Witnessed --

Japanese: 授業が終わって、リュウちゃんの もとへ参ろうと思いましたの。
English: I had been planning to go back to Feenie's place after class was over.

Japanese: 裏庭で、リュウちゃんが‥‥ ノンちゃんと、お話ししてました。
English: Feenie and Dougie... They were talking behind the building.

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥急に、ノンちゃんが おヨロめきになって‥‥お倒れに。
English: Then suddenly... Dougie got all wobbly and just collapsed.

Japanese: そのとき、リュウちゃんも わたしに気がついて。
English: That's when Feenie noticed that I was there.

Japanese: わたし、学生さんを呼びに行って ‥‥通報していただきました。
English: I went to go and find some other students and they called the authorities.

Japanese: ‥‥な‥‥なんですか、これは！ この証言によると‥‥
English: I-I don't know what to say! According to you, Ms. Hawthorne...

Japanese: 被告人は、何もしていないでは ないですか！
English: ...the defendant didn't do anything wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥おじょうさん。
English: Young lady.

Japanese: コイビトを想うアツぅい気持ちは この私にも、おぼえがありますぞ。
English: As old as I am, even I recall how hot the flames of young passion can burn.

Japanese: しかし‥‥ワレワレは、真実を 見つけなければなりません。
English: Nevertheless... It is my job to discover the truth.

Japanese: どうか、正直にホントのコトを‥‥
English: Please, tell us the truth...

Japanese: え。え。‥‥でも、 わたし、そんな。ウソなんて‥‥
English: But... But, I... I would never...

Japanese: ‥‥もうじゅうぶんです。証人。
English: That's more than enough, witness. I won't allow this to continue.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですかな？
English: Wh-What do you mean by that?

Japanese: とにかく、尋問をさせてください。
English: Please, just let me proceed with my cross-examination, Your Honor.

Japanese: ヤワなウソで、無罪を 勝ち取るつもりはありませんから。
English: I, for one, don't plan to win my case on a bunch of paper-thin lies!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥ふふふ。 あいかわらず、ですのね。
English: ...Tee hee. You haven't changed a bit...

Japanese: 綾里　千尋さん。
English: Mia Fey.

Japanese: お、オヤ。‥‥おふたりは 知り合いだったのですか‥‥？
English: What's this? So, you two are... acquainted?

Japanese: ええ。 ‥‥以前、ちょっと‥‥。
English: Yes. We've met before... Once.

Japanese: とにかく、弁護人。 ‥‥あなたにお任せしましょう。
English: In any case, Ms. Fey, the floor is all yours.

Japanese: よろしくおねがいしますね。 ‥‥おばさま。
English: It's good to see you again, Madame Fey!

Japanese: ダレがオバサマよっ！
English: "Madame"!? (I'm no one's grandma yet, girlie!)

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Witnessed --

Japanese: 授業が終わって、リュウちゃんの もとへ参ろうと思いましたの。
English: I had been planning to go back to Feenie's place after class was over.

Japanese: 裏庭で、リュウちゃんが‥‥ ノンちゃんと、お話ししてました。
English: Feenie and Dougie... They were talking behind the building.

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥急に、ノンちゃんが おヨロめきになって‥‥お倒れに。
English: Then suddenly... Dougie got all wobbly and just collapsed.

Japanese: そのとき、リュウちゃんも わたしに気がついて。
English: That's when Feenie noticed that I was there.

Japanese: わたし、学生さんを呼びに行って ‥‥通報していただきました。
English: I went to go and find some other students and they called the authorities.

Japanese: （あの子‥‥ワザとミエミエの ウソをついている）
English: (That girl... She's telling a super obvious lie, and she knows it.)

Japanese: （そう。なるほどくんを “守るフリ”をするために‥‥）
English: (She's just pretending to protect Mr. Wright...)

Japanese: （‥‥いいわね、千尋！）
English: (Yes, that's got to be it! Way to go, Mia!)

Japanese: （見抜くべきは、そのウラにある、 “ホントのムジュン”‥‥！）
English: (OK, that means I'm going to have to dig deep to find the contradiction on this one!)

Japanese: あの。わたし‥‥つきとばした 瞬間は、見ておりませんの。
English: Um, actually, I didn't see the moment he pushed Dougie.

Japanese: 争っているように見えなかったし、 なんの物音もしませんでしたから。
English: It didn't look like they were fighting, and I didn't hear any noise either.

Japanese: リュウちゃ‥‥なるほどくんは、 たしか、芸術学部でしたね。
English: Now, unless I'm mistaken, Feenie -- I mean, Mr. Wright is in the Art Department.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥なぜ、薬学部の 裏庭を通りかかったのかしら？
English: If that's the case, then what were you doing by the Pharmacology building?

Japanese: あの‥‥わたし、文学部で川柳を お勉強してるんですけれど。
English: Well... I'm in the Literature Department. I'm studying Japanese Senryu poetry.

Japanese: ほおほお。いいですなあ、川柳。
English: Oho hoh! How wonderful! It's that humorous, yet satirical style of haiku, yes?

Japanese: 『上がらない　ウダツこづかい 肩アタマ』‥‥のような。
English: "Nothing left to do... When a man reaches this age, sleep is his best friend."

Japanese: （中年のボヤキじゃない！）
English: (That's supposed to be poetry? Sounds more like a mid-life crisis!)

Japanese: 芸術学部の校舎へ行こうとすると、 あの裏庭を通ることになるんです。
English: For me to get to the Art Department, I have to walk through that back area.

Japanese: ふむふむ、なるほど。
English: Ah, yes I see. That makes sense.

Japanese: 私も、法廷に来るときはいつも、 裁判所の入り口を通りますからな。
English: When I want to enter the courthouse, I always walk through the front doors.

Japanese: （アタリマエでしょ！）
English: (How else would you enter? Teleportation!?)

Japanese: “ノンちゃん”というのは、 やっぱり‥‥？
English: So who is this "Dougie" person?

Japanese: ええ。呑田　菊三さん。 半年前まで、おつきあいを‥‥
English: Oh, I'm sorry. Doug Swallow. We were dating until about eight months ago...

Japanese: ノンちゃ‥‥呑田さんは なるほどくんと、どんなハナシを？
English: So what were Dougie-- *ahem* Mr. Swallow and Mr. Wright talking about anyway?

Japanese: ひ、ヒドいですわ！
English: H-How can you be so mean?

Japanese: わたし‥‥ヒトさまのハナシを 盗み聞きするような‥‥
English: I would never... I would never eavesdrop...

Japanese: そんな、はしたない子じゃ ありませんのよ！
English: I wasn't raised to be so rude and unrefined!

Japanese: そうですぞ、弁護人！
English: That's right, Ms. Fey!

Japanese: あなたといっしょに しないように！
English: Don't drag the witness down to your level!

Japanese: （うぐぐぐ‥‥）
English: (Why am I being demonized here?)

Japanese: そして‥‥そのあと、あなたは ナニを見たのですかな？
English: Please, go on. What did you see next?

Japanese: 被害者が‥‥自分で勝手に 倒れたというのですか？
English: Are you saying that the victim just collapsed on his own?

Japanese: え‥‥ええ。
English: Y-Yes.

Japanese: 被告人は、被害者に触れてもいない ‥‥そういうことかしら？
English: In other words, the defendant never touched the victim. Is that right?

Japanese: わたし‥‥拝見してましたから。 リュウちゃんは、何もしてません！
English: I was watching the whole time. Feenie never did a thing to Dougie!

Japanese: （イミもなくゆさぶったら、 まちがいなく怒られるわね‥‥）
English: (If I press her for no good reason, I just know the judge will get angry with me...)

Japanese: （‥‥どうかしら？　今の証言は）
English: (Hmm, so what should I do about her testimony just now?)

Japanese: （そうね‥‥どちらかといえば、 私たちに有利な証言だし‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I suppose her statement works in our favor, for now.)

Japanese: （深く追求するのは とりあえず、やめましょう）
English: (I'll hold off on looking into it any deeper until it's necessary.)

Japanese: それでは、おじょうさん。 証言をつづけてくださいな。
English: Very well, young lady, please go on with your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥見えすいたウソは やめてもらおうかしら。
English: Feeble lies are not very becoming, Ms. Hawthorne, so let's drop them, shall we?

Japanese: え‥‥！　‥‥そ、 そんな。わたし‥‥
English: Wh-What...! I...! I would never...!

Japanese: 弁護人！　この証人をイジめると、 ショウチしませんぞ！
English: Ms. Fey! I will not allow you to badger this witness!

Japanese: い、異議があるのなら、 まず‥‥コンキョをですな。
English: I-I believe the defense is engaged in a-a... fishing expedition.

Japanese: コンキョを、その‥‥
English: That is... Err... She has no supporting...

Japanese: そそそ、そんなに 見つめないでいただきたいッ！
English: P-Please don't glare at me like that...! I-I'm just doing my job!

Japanese: いいかしら、ちなみさん。
English: Now then, Ms. Hawthorne.

Japanese: 呑田さんの革ジャケットには、 被告人の指紋が残っていたのよ。
English: The defendant's palmprint was found on Mr. Swallow's leather jacket.

Japanese: なるほどくんがつきとばしたのは、 すでに立証されているの。
English: It has already been shown that Mr. Wright did, in fact, push the victim.
What...?

Japanese: ‥‥ムリに被告人を かばうことはないわ。
English: There's no need to try to cover for the defendant.

Japanese: それより‥‥真実を 証言してもらいたいわね。
English: It would be much better if you came out and told us the whole truth.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 大丈夫ですから、 真実をぶちまけてごらんなさい。
English: There's nothing to worry about, young lady. Just tell us everything that you saw.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥わかりました。 証言を、変えさせていただきます。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor... I-I will. If you don't mind, I-I'd like to revise my testimony.

Japanese: （これで‥‥どうやら 一歩前進ね、千尋‥‥）
English: (Looks like we're finally getting somewhere...)

Japanese: そのとき、なるほどくんと どんな会話を？
English: And what did Mr. Wright say when he saw you?

Japanese: すみません。わたし‥‥ホントに とりみだしてしまって。
English: I-I'm sorry. I-I was so flustered that I...

Japanese: よく、おぼえておりませんの。 本当に、もうしわけございません。
English: I really don't remember. P-Please forgive me.

Japanese: おぼえていない‥‥？
English: You don't remember...?

Japanese: ふむふむ。よくあるコトですな。
English: Well, that's common enough.

Japanese: 私も、さっき下した判決を おぼえてないコトがありますぞ！
English: Sometimes I can't recall a sentence I passed only minutes prior!

Japanese: （‥‥おねがい。だれか、 あのおジイさんを止めて‥‥）
English: (Please... Someone, anyone... Stop him before he gets hurt... by me.)

Japanese: “学生さん”というのは、 薬学部の‥‥？
English: When you say "students", do you mean students from the Pharmacology Department?

Japanese: はい。みなさん、とても おクスリ好きな方たちで‥‥
English: Yes. They're all very fond of their drugs...

Japanese: それはカンケイないでしょう！
English: Please try to stay on topic!

Japanese: 薬学部の学生、というと‥‥ 研究室に呼びに行ったのかしら？
English: So to find some Pharmacology students, you went to the labs, correct?

Japanese: そのつもりだったのですけれど‥‥ ケッキョク、行きませんでしたわ。
English: That's what I was planning to do... But in the end, I wound up not going.

Japanese: 校舎の入り口で、１０人ぐらいの 研究グループの方が走ってきて。
English: A group of about 10 research students came running out of the building entrance.

Japanese: どうやらみなさん、事件のコトを 知っていらっしゃったような‥‥
English: Somehow they all seemed to know what was going on...

Japanese: （‥‥学生たちが、 事件を知っていた‥‥？）
English: (The students knew what was going on...?)

Japanese: （薬学部の学生‥‥ 事件には関係ないわ）
English: (The Pharmacology students... They have nothing to do with the incident.)

Japanese: （もっと、彼女自身の情報を 引き出したほうがいいわね）
English: (I think what I need is more information about Dahlia herself.)

Japanese: その学生たちは、 どうして事件のコトを‥‥？
English: But how could the students have known what was going on?

Japanese: あの‥‥あの方たちが本当に事件を 知っていたのかは、わかりません。
English: Well... I don't know for sure that they knew what had happened.

Japanese: ただ‥‥みなさん、血相を お変えになっていたから‥‥。
English: It's just, they all seemed kind of excited about something.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥学生たちに関して、 これ以上の情報はないみたいね）
English: (Hmm... It doesn't look like I'm going to get any more info about the students.)

Japanese: 警察への通報は、彼らが？
English: So did the students call the police?

Japanese: え、ええ。わたし、もう‥‥ おムネがドキドキしてしまって。
English: Y-Yes. I-I was just so... I was so panicked...

Japanese: ふむふむ。そうでしょう。 おムネがねえ。
English: Hmm. Yes, well anyone would have been, my dear.

Japanese: “見ていない”‥‥？
English: You didn't see it!?

Japanese: ノンちゃんが、お倒れになった ところは拝見しましたけれど。
English: Well, I saw the moment when Dougie fell to the ground...

Japanese: そのとき、現場には ふたりしかいなかったのですな？
English: And at that time, there was only the two of them at the scene?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一と、 被害者の呑田　菊三と。
English: The defendant, Phoenix Wright, and the victim, Doug Swallow?

Japanese: それは‥‥はい。 おっしゃるとおりです。
English: Yes... that's right.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥あなたには どう見えたのかしら？
English: So then, what did it look like they were doing to you?

Japanese: それはなごやかに、午後の会話を 楽しんでいらっしゃるのかと。
English: I thought they were having a nice, friendly afternoon conversation.

Japanese: （そんなワケないでしょっ！）
English: (Oh, give me a break!)

Japanese: だからわたし、おふたりを ちゃんと見ていなかったんです。
English: That's why I really wasn't watching them all that closely.

Japanese: ほかに何か、変わったことは なかったのですかな？
English: Did you notice anything out of the ordinary at all?

Japanese: ありませんでしたの。 おじさま‥‥。
English: No, nothing at all... Mr. Judge.

Japanese: うんうん。いいヒビキですねえ ‥‥オジサマ。
English: Oh, I like the sound of that! "Mr. Judge"...

Japanese: では、証言をつづけてください。
English: Now then, please proceed with your testimony.

Japanese: “なんの物音もしなかった”‥‥ そう言いましたね？
English: You say you didn't hear any noise. Is that correct?

Japanese: はい。だから安心して、 よそ見をしておりましたの。
English: Yes. That's why I was very relaxed, looking at the scenery around me.

Japanese: ‥‥それはおかしいのよ。
English: That's nice, but I find that just a little odd.

Japanese: ここに、あなたのコイビト 成歩堂　龍一の証言書があるわ。
English: I have here, the testimony of your boyfriend, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 彼は、ハッキリ こう証言しています。
English: And he clearly testified to the effect...

Japanese: 『被害者をつきとばした瞬間、 鋭く大きな音がした』と。
English: ...that when he pushed the victim, he heard a sharp, loud noise.

Japanese: 大きな‥‥“音”‥‥
English: H-He said that?

Japanese: 近くにいたあなたが、なぜ‥‥ この音を聞いていないのかしら？
English: If you were really that close to the two of them, why didn't you hear this noise as well?

Japanese: そ、そんな音など、印象に 残らなかったかもしれませんぞ！
English: W-Well, maybe the noise just wasn't all that memorable.

Japanese: なるほどくんの証言によれば、 “バチッ”という鋭い音でした。
English: But according to Mr. Wright's testimony, it was a sharp noise like a "SNAP"!

Japanese: 印象に残らないハズはありません！
English: There's no way a noise like that could fail to make an impression!

Japanese: ぎゃうッ！
English: Ack!!

Japanese: ‥‥あの‥‥ お待ちいただけますかしら。
English: Umm... May I have a moment to answer?

Japanese: ム！　なんですかな！
English: B-By all means!

Japanese: わたしが音を聞いていないのは‥‥ ちゃんと、理由がありますの。
English: I know the reason why I didn't hear the noise.

Japanese: じつは、わたし‥‥
English: You see, the truth is...

Japanese: ヘッドフォンで お歌を聞いていたんです。
English: I had my headphones on, and I was listening to music at the time.

Japanese: へ、ヘッドフォン‥‥ 両耳がふさがっていたワケですか？
English: H-H-Headphones...? You mean that both of your ears were covered?

Japanese: あのとき、雨は やみかけていたんですけれど‥‥
English: The rain was just beginning to let up...

Japanese: カミナリさまが、お暴れに なっていたんです。
English: But it seemed as though Thor wasn't ready for his fun to come to an end yet.

Japanese: ‥‥ごろごろごろ、って。
English: So the sky continued to flash and rumble.

Japanese: カミナリさま‥‥？
English: Thunder and lightning, huh...

Japanese: わたし、カミナリさまが ニガテなんです。
English: Yes, I'm afraid of the sound of thunder.

Japanese: だから、 それが聞こえないように、って‥‥
English: So I put my headphones on to block it out...

Japanese: うふふふふふ‥‥ いかがですかな、裁判長。
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee... Well, Your Honor?

Japanese: 証人の証言には、これで ムジュンはなくなりましたぞ！
English: As you can see, there weren't any contradictions in her testimony after all.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （ちょっと待って、千尋！ 今の証言には‥‥）
English: (Wait a sec, Mia! That testimony just now...)

Japanese: （事件の流れをひっくり返すような 重大なポイントがあったわ！）
English: (She said something that could totally change this whole case!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　証人の証言には １つ、問題があります！
English: Your Honor! There is a problem with the witness's testimony!

Japanese: な、なんですと！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: い、いったいそれは‥‥？
English: Wh-What is this "problem"!?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥証人が“バチッ”と いう音を、聞かなかったコトです！
English: It's obvious. The problem is that the witness claims to have not heard the "SNAP!"

Japanese: なな、ナニを聞いていたのですか！
English: A-Are you deaf!? Did you pay attention to the testimony!?

Japanese: その理由は説明されたでしょう！ “ヘッドフォンで歌を聞いていた”
English: She said she didn't hear it because she was listening to music with her headphones!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 私より先にボケないように。
English: Ms. Fey! I'm surprised you're going senile before me!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥）
English: (I'm not THAT bad! ...I think.)

Japanese: 裁判長！　証人の証言には １つ、問題があります！
English: Your Honor! There is a problem with the witness's testimony!

Japanese: な、なんですと！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: い、いったいそれは‥‥？
English: Wh-What is this "problem"!?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥証人が お歌を聞いていたコトです！
English: It's obvious. The problem is that she claims to have been listening to music!

Japanese: なな、ナニを聞いていたのですか！
English: A-Are you deaf!? Did you pay attention to the testimony!?

Japanese: その理由は説明されたでしょう！ “カミナリがコワかった”
English: She explained she was listening to music to cover up the sound of thunder!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 私のカミナリもコワいですぞ！
English: Ms. Fey! I must warn you that I can be even more frightening than Zeus when I'm angry!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥）
English: (Yikes! I'm in trouble now!)

Japanese: 今の証言で‥‥どうやら 結論が出てしまったようですな。
English: Well, the testimony we've just heard seems to be very decisive.

Japanese: 被害者を殺害できたのは ‥‥やはり、被告人しかいない！
English: The only one that could have killed the victim was the defendant, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥わたし、 そんなつもりでは‥‥！
English: N-No! Please...! I-I never meant to incriminate him!

Japanese: （だ、ダメよ千尋！ 終わっちゃうじゃない、裁判！）
English: (This is bad, Mia! If you don't do something, we're going to lose this!!)

Japanese: （もう一度、考えて！ 今の証言には‥‥）
English: (Think again! What is it about this testimony that doesn't make sense...?)

Japanese: 裁判長！　証人の証言には １つ、問題があります！
English: Your Honor! There is a problem with the witness's testimony!

Japanese: な、なんですと！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: 気になりませんか？ ‥‥“カミナリ”ですよ？
English: Didn't you notice? She said there was lightning, correct?

Japanese: それが、どうかしましたか？
English: Yes... What about it?

Japanese: “カミナリ”って、 じつは電気なんですよ？
English: Well, lightning is actually a large discharge of electricity in the atmosphere, am I right?

Japanese: それぐらい知っていますッ！
English: Now's not the time for a science lesson, Ms. Fey!

Japanese: 被害者の死因は“感電”。 まず考えられるのが‥‥
English: Yes, Your Honor. Anyway, since the cause of death was electrocution...

Japanese: “落雷”じゃないですか！
English: ...isn't it possible that the victim died from being hit by a bolt of lightning!?

Japanese: ‥‥あッ！
English: Oh!

Japanese: ‥‥おッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: ううむ‥‥さすがの私も、 そこまでは気づきませんでした！
English: Hmm... I must admit that the thought had not occurred to me!

Japanese: （‥‥どこまでなら 気づけるって言うのよ‥‥）
English: (Just what kind of thoughts DO occur to this guy anyway?)

Japanese: ‥‥呑田　菊三が “殺害された”‥‥
English: This entire case is built on the premise that Mr. Doug Swallow was "murdered"...

Japanese: そもそも、その前提が まちがっていた！
English: ...but that very premise itself is mistaken!

Japanese: 被害者は‥‥落雷による “事故”で死んだのですっ！
English: The defense believes that Mr. Swallow was, in fact, the victim of a stray bolt!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに、 弁護人の言うとおりのようです！
English: I-It appears the defense may be on to something!

Japanese: まさか、この被害者が‥‥ 事故死だったとはッ！
English: Could it be that the death was actually accidental?

Japanese: （や‥‥やったわ！　千尋！）
English: (Alright, you did it, Mia!)

Japanese: （これで、無罪判決はイタダキ‥‥
English: (I'll be taking that "Not Guilty" now if you don't--)

Japanese: ‥‥うふふふふふふふふふ‥‥
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee, hee...

Japanese: ワレワレを ナメてもらっては困りますなあ。
English: I'm hurt that you have such a low opinion of me, Ms. Fey.
Huh...?

Japanese: 検察側は、そこまでバカじゃない。 気象報告は調べましたよ。
English: I'm not a fool, you know. The prosecution has done its research, Your Honor.

Japanese: 事件当日‥‥。現場付近に 落雷はありませんでした！
English: We found that there were no lightning strikes on that day at that location!

Japanese: ええええッ！
English: Whaaa!?

Japanese: それに‥‥
English: What's more...

Japanese: この送電線が、事件に関係している という証拠もあるのです。
English: We have evidence that the electrical cable is definitely linked to this case.

Japanese: 亜内検事！ そ、それはいったい‥‥？
English: E-Evidence, Mr. Payne!? Well, what is this evidence?

Japanese: ‥‥この、証言書です。
English: This affidavit.

Japanese: “証言書”‥‥ですか。 いったい、どなたの？
English: And who is this affidavit from?

Japanese: 事件当時、薬学部の研究室で 実験をしていた学生たちです。
English: The Pharmacology students who were conducting experiments in their labs that day.

Japanese: 彼らは、こう証言しております。 “あの日の午後３時ごろ”‥‥
English: Allow me to read out to the court the testimony of the Pharmacology students.

Japanese: “いきなり、すべての実験機器が 停止してしまった”‥‥と！
English: "All equipment in the labs lost power all of a sudden at around 3:00 PM that day."

Japanese: ‥‥停電、ですか‥‥
English: Was it a blackout?

Japanese: 薬学部の実験機器は、高圧電流を 使用するものばかりでした。
English: All of the labs' equipment runs on high-voltage, Your Honor.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥、 それらの実験機器の電力は‥‥？
English: So you're saying the equipment lost power because...

Japanese: さよう。この、切れた電線によって まかなわれていたのですよ。
English: Precisely. They lost power because of the severed electrical cable.

Japanese: そして、その実験機器が止まった 時刻は、午後３時ごろ‥‥
English: The power outage occurred at approximately 3:00 PM...

Japanese: 解剖記録の死亡推定時刻と、 ピッタリ一致しますな。
English: Which fits with the time of death listed in the autopsy report.

Japanese: そのとおり！　つまり‥‥
English: Exactly! In other words...

Japanese: 被害者は、この切れた送電線に 触れて、ショック死したのです！
English: The victim died as a result of touching the severed electrical cable.

Japanese: 学生たちによれば、この送電線は そうとう古くなっていて‥‥
English: According to the students, the cables were very old.

Japanese: 近いうちに、改修の工事が 予定されていたそうです。
English: They were planning on having them replaced in the near future.

Japanese: そうだったのですか‥‥
English: Hmm, I see...

Japanese: 何か少しでもショックがあれば、 いつでも切れる状態にあった‥‥
English: Apparently the cables had become so brittle...

Japanese: そう考えていいようです。
English: ...that even the smallest bump would've caused them to break.

Japanese: 《学生たちの証言書》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Students' Testimony added to the Court Record.

Japanese: しかし‥‥そうなると、 気になることがあります。
English: However... There is one thing that troubles me.

Japanese: “送電線は、少しのショックで 切れる状態だった”‥‥つまり、
English: If the cable could have been broken by any small bump...

Japanese: “ショックがなければ、送電線は 切れなかった”ということです。
English: ...then it wouldn't have snapped if it hadn't been bumped into, correct?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥たしかに、 それはそうですな。
English: W-Well... I suppose you could say that.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。いかがですか。
English: Hmm, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: 送電線が切れた原因について、 何か、考えはありますかな？
English: Do you have any thoughts regarding the cause of the severed cable?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-Your Honor...

Japanese: （今‥‥こっちの状況は、 かなり不利だわ）
English: (I don't like how this is looking one bit!)

Japanese: （反撃するためには‥‥ ここで何か、しかけないと！）
English: (I have to come up with something to try to regain some momentum!)

Japanese: わかりました。弁護側の 考えを言わせていただきます。
English: If it pleases the court, the defense would like to state its opinion.

Japanese: それでは、 提示していただきましょう！
English: Well then, let's hear it.

Japanese: なぜ送電線が切れたか‥‥ “ショック”の原因を作ったのは？
English: Who or what was it that caused the cable to break?

Japanese: ‥‥これこそが、 電線がプッツリ切れた理由です！
English: This is it! This is why the cable snapped.

Japanese: 裁判長、いかがですかな？
English: Well, Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥プッツリ切れたのは、 弁護人の生命線、のようですな。
English: I believe that the only thing that has snapped is the mind of the defense.

Japanese: さすが裁判長どの。 ウマいことをおっしゃる。
English: Ah, that was one of your best lines yet, Your Honor.

Japanese: う‥‥ッ！ （くく、クヤシイ‥‥）
English: Nnnfff...! (Ouch, that was harsh...)

Japanese: も、もう一度、 おねがいします！
English: Please, Your Honor! Give me one more chance!

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥なるほどくんの 証言を思い出してください。
English: Your Honor. Please think back to Mr. Wright's testimony.

Japanese: 被告人、ですか‥‥？
English: The defendant's testimony?

Japanese: 彼は、被害者をつきとばしたとき、 大きな鋭い音を聞いています。
English: He said that after he pushed the victim, he heard a loud, sharp noise.

Japanese: “バチッ”という音を。 それは、午後３時ごろでした。
English: Now, this happened at around 3:00 PM, correct?

Japanese: たしかに、そうでしたな。 ‥‥ま、まさか‥‥！
English: Yes, that sounds right. Wait! Are you saying that...

Japanese: 実験機器が停止した時刻が２時 ５５分。‥‥証言とも一致します。
English: The lab equipment lost power at 2:55 PM... which fits right in Mr. Wright's timeline.

Japanese: 送電線を切ったのは‥‥ なるほどくんの一撃だったのです。
English: In other words, it was Mr. Wright's shove that caused the power outage.

Japanese: さよう！ それは、検察側も同意見ですな。
English: Yes! The prosecution also came to that same conclusion.

Japanese: そして、被告人の一撃で、 呑田くんは感電死した！
English: And it was that very shove that caused Mr. Swallow to be electrocuted!

Japanese: そうはいきません。
English: I'm afraid I can't agree with you there, Mr. Payne.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと？
English: Wh-What's that supposed to mean?

Japanese: なるほどくんが、呑田さんを つきとばした“場所”は‥‥
English: Take a good look at where the victim landed after being shoved.

Japanese: カサが落ちている地点。 ‥‥つまり、電柱のあたりです。
English: See the umbrella? It's by the electrical pole.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。つきとばされた 被害者は、この電柱に激突した。
English: That's right. The victim banged into that pole as a result of being pushed.

Japanese: そのときのショックで‥‥ 送電線が切れたのです。
English: It was that impact that caused the cable to break.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。それならば、 カンゼンにスジはとおりますな。
English: Hmm... Well, that makes sense. And then the victim was electrocuted.

Japanese: おコトバですが‥‥ まったく、スジはとおりません。
English: I'm sorry Your Honor, but no. It doesn't make sense at all.

Japanese: 被害者が、奥の電柱に向かって つきとばされたのならば‥‥
English: If the victim was shoved into the far pole...

Japanese: この切れた送電線で、被害者が 感電できるハズがありません！
English: ...then he couldn't have been electrocuted by this severed cable in the foreground here!

Japanese: なぎゃあああああああああああッ！
English: AAAAAAAAAAHH!!

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: In other words...

Japanese: 被害者を感電死させたのは、 別の人物ということになるのです！
English: Someone other than my client must have electrocuted the victim!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛にいいッ！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: （ああ。敵の悲鳴って‥‥なんて 心地よく、ムネにヒビくの！）
English: (Ah, the lamentations of my enemy. How I've longed to hear them!)

Japanese: た、たしかに‥‥弁護人の主張は、 反論の余地がありません！
English: It-It's true... The defense is absolutely correct!

Japanese: 被告人が、呑田　菊三くんを 殺害できるはずが‥‥
English: There doesn't seem to be any way the defendant could have done it--

Japanese: あの‥‥おじさま？ よろしいかしら。
English: Umm... Mr. Judge, sir? May I say something?

Japanese: 今の、おばさまの説明‥‥
English: The madame attorney's explanation...

Japanese: わたしが拝見しましたものと 少々、ちがっているのですけれど。
English: She said some things that are a little different than I remember them.
Wh-Wh-
Wh-Wh-

Japanese: なんですってえぇっ！
English: What the...!?

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度だけ。
English: Please, just once more.

Japanese: 証言させていただけませんの？ ‥‥おじさま‥‥
English: May I please testify one last time? Please... Mr. Judge?

Japanese: モチロン、けっこうです！
English: Of course it's alright!

Japanese: サッソクもう一度、 証言していただきましょう！
English: Just go right ahead and give your new testimony!

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥）
English: (This is it...)

Japanese: （本性をあらわしてきたようね！）
English: (She's finally starting to show her true colors!)

Japanese: ～目撃したこと・２～
English: -- What I Witnessed, Part 2 --

Japanese: リュウちゃんは‥‥２回、 おつきとばしになったのです。
English: The truth is... Feenie pushed him twice.

Japanese: １回目、電柱にぶつけられて‥‥ 送電線が、お切れになりました。
English: The first time was into the electrical pole. That's when the cable broke.

Japanese: それで、ノンちゃんは必死に お逃げになろうとしたのですが‥‥
English: Then Dougie tried his best to run away from him...

Japanese: リュウちゃんは、すぐに追いかけて 背中から、そっと体当たりを。
English: But Feenie caught up and crashed into him from behind.

Japanese: 送電線が切れてから、お感電まで、 １分もたっておりませんでしたわ。
English: The cable snapping, and Dougie being electrocuted -- it all occured in less than a minute.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。被告人に つきとばされた被害者は‥‥
English: Hmm... So after being shoved...

Japanese: 起き上がって、送電線のほうへ 逃げたわけですか。
English: ...the victim got up and tried to run away.

Japanese: ‥‥そこを、ふたたび 被告人に倒された。
English: And that is when the defendant pushed him for the second time.

Japanese: リュウちゃんには悪いけど‥‥ ホントのコトを言わなきゃ、って。
English: I'm so sorry, Feenie... But I... I just have to tell the truth.

Japanese: これでよろしいのですよね？ ‥‥おじさま。
English: Am I doing the right thing? Am I, Mr. Judge?

Japanese: いやはや。マッタクもって、 そのとおりです！
English: Of course you are, my dear! As painful as it may seem, you are.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいしましょう！
English: Now then, Ms. Fey. You may proceed with your cross-examination!

Japanese: ～目撃したこと・２～
English: -- What I Witnessed, Part 2 --

Japanese: リュウちゃんは‥‥２回、 おつきとばしになったのです。
English: The truth is... Feenie pushed him twice.

Japanese: １回目、電柱にぶつけられて‥‥ 送電線が、お切れになりました。
English: The first time was into the electrical pole. That's when the cable broke.

Japanese: それで、ノンちゃんは必死に お逃げになろうとしたのですが‥‥
English: Then Dougie tried his best to run away from him...

Japanese: リュウちゃんは、すぐに追いかけて 背中から、そっと体当たりを。
English: But Feenie caught up and crashed into him from behind.

Japanese: 送電線が切れてから、お感電まで、 １分もたっておりませんでしたわ。
English: The cable snapping, and Dougie being electrocuted -- it all occured in less than a minute.

Japanese: （ここで決定的なムジュンを 見つけないと‥‥）
English: (If I don't figure out the contradiction here...)

Japanese: （まちがいなく、おしまいよ！）
English: (...it's all over!)

Japanese: （あの子にも、ウソを考える時間は ほとんどなかったはず）
English: (She didn't have much time to come up with her lie, so this is my best chance.)

Japanese: （どこかに必ず、 穴があるわ‥‥千尋！）
English: (There must be a hole in her testimony somewhere! Think, Mia!)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥あなたはさっき、 こう証言したはずよ。
English: Ms. Hawthorne... Previously in your testimony you said the following...

Japanese: “つきとばした瞬間は見ていない” ‥‥って。
English: "Actually, I didn't see the moment he pushed Dougie."

Japanese: ごめんなさい。わたし‥‥ リュウちゃんを守らなきゃ、って。
English: I know... I-I'm sorry. I wanted to protect Feenie.

Japanese: だから、ウソをついたと いうワケかしら？
English: So that's why you basically lied to the court?

Japanese: ちなみ、いけない子でした。 ‥‥あの、おじさま？
English: I was a bad girl... I know... Um, Mr. Judge?

Japanese: ム！　なんですかな？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: こんなちなみを‥‥ ユルしてはいただけませんの？
English: Would you please, please forgive little old me?

Japanese: アタリマエですっ！　あなたが したことは“偽証”といって‥‥
English: Of course he won't! What you did is called "perjury"...

Japanese: いやいや！　オジサマはいつだって あなたの味方ですぞ！
English: Oh, come now! It was just a little old white lie!

Japanese: 今回だけは、サービスしましょう！ 次から、気をつけるように。
English: We'll forget it this time! But please be more careful from now on, alright?

Japanese: まあ、ウレシイ‥‥ ありがとう、おじさまっ。
English: Oh, thank you so much, Mr. Judge.

Japanese: なんのなんの。ほっほっほっ。
English: Not at all! Ho ho ho...!

Japanese: （‥‥裁判長。暗い夜道には 気をつけるコトね‥‥）
English: (The judge had better be more careful himself! A dark alley is friendlier than that girl!)

Japanese: 被害者が電柱にぶつかった‥‥ それを目撃したのかしら？
English: You're saying you actually saw the victim get pushed into the electrical pole?

Japanese: リュウちゃんって、ああ見えて 意外にワンパクですのよ。
English: I know he doesn't look it, but Feenie can be a bit of an imp when he wants to be.

Japanese: （ワンパク‥‥）
English: (Oh, really...?)

Japanese: でも、まさか送電線が お切れになるなんて‥‥。
English: But I never imagined that he would cause an electrical cable to break...

Japanese: リュウちゃんって、ホント やんちゃボウズさんですよね。
English: Feenie really is scary when he gets mad...

Japanese: まったく、困った やんちゃボウズさんですな。
English: Yes, he sounds like a very dangerous individual indeed.

Japanese: そんな光景を 目にしていながら‥‥
English: So let me get this straight.

Japanese: あなたは、ヘッドフォンで お歌を聞いていたワケですか？
English: You were happily listening to music on your headphones while you watched this scene unfold?
Waaaaah...

Japanese: 弁護人！　あてこすりは やめていただきたいですな！
English: Ms. Fey! I'll have to ask you to stop badgering the witness!

Japanese: あの‥‥わたし、コワくて すくんでしまったんです。
English: Umm... I wasn't happy... I was so scared that I couldn't even move.

Japanese: ココロの中で、おふたりを 応援することしかできなくて。
English: All I could do was stand there and cheer them on...

Japanese: お、おうえん‥‥？
English: Ch-Cheer them on? What do you mean by that?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。 “どちらもファイト！”‥‥って。
English: Well, I wished the best for them both, and that they would each give the fight their all.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。いじらしい おとめゴコロ、というヤツですな。
English: Hmm... That's very sweet of you to be so supportive.

Japanese: それで‥‥そのあと、 どうなりましたかな？
English: And what happened after that?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、おかしいですね。
English: That doesn't sound quite right...

Japanese: 被害者のジャケットのムネには、 たしかに、手の跡が残っています。
English: There were handprints found on the chest of the victim's leather jacket.

Japanese: 亜内検事。ジャケットの背中にも、 なるほどくんの指紋が‥‥？
English: Mr. Payne, were there also prints found on the back of his leather jacket?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ 残っていませんが‥‥
English: W-Well, um... N-No, there weren't...

Japanese: おばさま。ちなみのハナシ、 よく聞いていただけますかしら？
English: Madame Fey, may I suggest that you listen a little more carefully?

Japanese: リュウちゃんは“体当たり” なさったのですよ？
English: I said that he "crashed" into him from behind, right?

Japanese: 指紋なんて、お残りになるはず、 ありませんでしょう？
English: My Feenie wouldn't leave any prints behind in that case, would he?
Unngghh...

Japanese: あなたは‥‥被害者が感電死する 瞬間を目撃したわけですか？
English: Did you actually witness the moment the victim was electrocuted?

Japanese: スミマセン。わたし‥‥ 拝見していないのです。
English: I-I'm sorry. I didn't actually see it.

Japanese: ‥‥思わず、 目をそむけてしまって‥‥
English: I... I turned my eyes away...

Japanese: ‥‥ムリもありませんな。
English: That's understandable.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。きっと、かなり ビリビリ来たんでしょうなあ。
English: Yes indeed. It would have been a horrific sight for anyone to behold.

Japanese: ‥‥もうケッコウです、証人。
English: That's enough, witness.
I'm afraid I don't understand...

Japanese: ‥‥この写真を 見てもらえるかしら。
English: You will in a minute. Could you please take a look at this picture?

Japanese: あ。このおクスリ、 リュウちゃんがご愛飲していた‥‥
English: Oh. That medicine... That's the one Feenie likes to take for his cold.

Japanese: ここで重要なのは、 薬ビンではありません。
English: It's not the medicine I want you to look at...

Japanese: ‥‥ダイジなのは、 腕時計のほうなんです。
English: It's the wristwatch.

Japanese: この時計は、被害者が感電死した 時間に止まりました。
English: It stopped at the precise time the victim was electrocuted.

Japanese: つまり‥‥３時５分。 それが、感電の時間になります。
English: In other words... 3:05 PM.

Japanese: そのとおりですが‥‥
English: Yes, and your point is, Ms. Fey...?

Japanese: しかし！　思い出してください。
English: My point is this!

Japanese: “送電線が切れた”のは‥‥ いったい、何時だったか‥‥？
English: What time was it when the lab suffered that power outage due to the cable snapping?

Japanese: それは‥‥学生たちの証言で アキラカです。
English: Well, according to the students' testimony, the answer is clear.

Japanese: 午後２時５５分‥‥ あああああッ！
English: It was 2:55 PM... Yeeaaaaaaarrrgghhh!!

Japanese: ‥‥説明してもらえるかしら、 美柳　ちなみさん‥‥
English: Would you care to explain to the court, Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne...

Japanese: この、１０分間の《空白の時間》は いったい、なんなのか！
English: ...what exactly happened during this 10 minute interval!?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側は主張します！
English: The defense proposes that...

Japanese: 被害者を死に追いやった 真犯人は、他にいたのです！
English: ...it was during this interval that the real murderer killed Mr. Doug Swallow!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！ これはいったい‥‥
English: Or-Order! Order in the court! What is this all ab--

Japanese: で‥‥デタラメだッ！ し、“真犯人”などと‥‥！
English: Th-This is nonsense! The "real murderer"...!?

Japanese: 送電線が切れてから、被害者が 殺害されるまで、約１０分間！
English: Even you can't deny that the time between the cable break- ing and the electrocution...

Japanese: ‥‥空白の時間が存在するのは ジジツです！
English: ...are completely unaccounted for!

Japanese: いったい、ダレが！　被告人の 他に、ダレにできたと言うのです！
English: Th-Then who was it!? Who else are you saying could've done it!?

Japanese: たったひとりだけ‥‥ 存在するじゃないですか。
English: There's only one person who could have murdered Mr. Swallow.

Japanese: 被告人が呑田さんをつきとばして 現場を立ち去ったあと‥‥
English: Only after my client had left the scene...

Japanese: 彼を殺害できた人物が！
English: ...was there a window of opportunity for the real killer!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたは、 だれかを告発するつもりですか？
English: Ms. Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥真犯人として！
English: Is the defense ready to indict someone as this "real killer"?

Japanese: （いよいよ‥‥）
English: (It's finally time.)

Japanese: （いよいよ、 この時が来たわね‥‥千尋！）
English: (This is the moment I've been waiting for.)

Japanese: はい。そのつもりです。
English: Yes, Your Honor. We are ready.

Japanese: よろしい、うかがいましょう。 ‥‥ただし！
English: Very well. But remember!

Japanese: 無関係の人物を告発した場合は ペナルティを与えます。
English: If you accuse the wrong person, you will be penalized.

Japanese: よく考えるように！
English: Think very carefully before you speak, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: それでは‥‥あなたの考える “真犯人”を提示してください！
English: Now then, Ms. Fey. Let's have it. Who is the "real killer"!?

Japanese: なんですか、今のは。
English: What's that supposed to be!?

Japanese: え‥‥。その‥‥ 若さゆえの先走り、ですっ！
English: Err... Umm... A bout of youthful indiscretion! ...Your Honor!

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥ヤクソクどおり、 ペナルティを与えます。
English: Maybe so, but I still have to penalize you.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥ダメよ！ ここで立ち止まっちゃ‥‥）
English: (Unngggh... No way! I won't let it end like this!)

Japanese: もう一度、うかがいます。 今度こそ、よく考えるように。
English: Now this time, I expect you to think a bit more carefully!

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさん。 ‥‥もちろん、あなたしかいない！
English: It could only have been you, Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: え‥‥ そ、そんな‥‥
English: Waaaaah! H-How... H-How can you...!

Japanese: なな、何をバカなッ！
English: Th-The defense is grasping at straws!

Japanese: 送電線が切れてから、呑田さんが 感電死するまでの、１０分間！
English: Ten minutes passed between the time the cable broke and the time of the electrocution!

Japanese: その間、あなたは 何をしていたのですか？
English: What exactly were you doing that whole time, Ms. Hawthorne?

Japanese: 助けも呼ばず、ヘッドフォンで お歌を聞きながら‥‥
English: Were you really listening to some music while "cheering them both on" as they fought?

Japanese: “どちらもファイト！”と 応援していたのかしらね！
English: I find it hard to believe that you didn't lift a finger to stop the men "dearest" to you!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥弁護人ッ！
English: Order! Order! Ms. Fey!

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥いったいその。 どういうあの。そういうこの。
English: Wh-What... I mean, why... That is to say...

Japanese: 証人！　あなたはあの日、 ふたりの言い争いを目撃した！
English: Ms. Hawthorne! I believe you did witness the two men fighting on that day. However!

Japanese: そして、なるほどくんが被害者を つきとばして立ち去ったあと‥‥
English: After Mr. Wright pushed the victim, and subsequently left the scene...

Japanese: あなた自身が、その手で 彼をつきとばし、殺害したのです！
English: ...it was YOU who pushed Mr. Swallow to his death by your very own hands!

Japanese: ‥‥きゃあああああああッ！
English: AAAAAAAAHHH!!!

Japanese: ヒドい‥‥おばさま。 ちなみ、なんにもしてないのに‥‥
English: H-How can you say something so mean, Madame Fey...! I... I didn't do anything...!

Japanese: 弁護人ッ！　これは、重大な‥‥
English: Ms. Fey! This is a very serious charge you are--

Japanese: この裁判、ちょっと待ったあッ！
English: Your Honor! P-Please, I have something I want to say!

Japanese: ‥‥げほ。
English: *achoo*

Japanese: な、な、な、な、な‥‥ なんですか！　あなたはッ！
English: Y-Y-You! Wh-What is it!?

Japanese: 弁護側は‥‥今の発言を 全面的に取り消しますッ！
English: Please... Please strike everything the defense said just now from the record!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカなコト言わないで！
English: What the...!? Are you daft!?

Japanese: 千尋さん、見そこなったよッ！
English: You're totally wrong, Miss Fey!

Japanese: ちいちゃんは‥‥ちいちゃんは、
English: Dollie... She...

Japanese: そんなコトしないやいっ！
English: She couldn't do something like that!

Japanese: コラコラ！　勝手に被告席から 出てきちゃダメでしょう！
English: Mr. Wright!! Get back in your seat!

Japanese: 法廷係官！　ちゃんと つかまえておくように！
English: Bailiff! Grab that man!

Japanese: うおおえええ‥‥げほげほ。 ちいちゃんをイジめるな！　げほ。
English: Aaaarrgghh... *achoo* *achoo* Leave my Dollie alone! *achoo* *cough*

Japanese: ‥‥ヤレヤレ。
English: *sigh* That boy...

Japanese: あのワカモノには、あまり 関わらんほうがよさそうぢゃの。
English: He's gotten himself in way over his head.

Japanese: ほ‥‥星影先生っ！
English: Oh...! Mr. Grossberg! You're back!

Japanese: なんとか間に合ったようぢゃな。
English: It seems I've arrived just in the nick of time.

Japanese: チミの新聞記事にあった事件‥‥ 警察の資料を手に入れてきたぞい！
English: I found the police report on that incident in your newspaper clipping!

Japanese: 証拠品《警察の資料》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Police Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。 ‥‥この記録がほしかったんです。
English: Thank you so much! This is exactly what I was hoping for!

Japanese: ‥‥よく目を通しておくといい。
English: You'd better take a good look at it...

Japanese: チミが、その‥‥コイビトを 失った事件。その記録を、な。
English: It... err... details how you came to lose your boyfriend.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。弁護側から、 重大な告発がありました。
English: Now then, the defense has made a very serious accusation.

Japanese: 亜内検事。いかがですか。
English: Mr. Payne, what do you have to say about this?

Japanese: こ、これはもう、その‥‥
English: W-Well really, Your Honor... I... I... That is I...

Japanese: ちょっといいですか？ 弁護士さん。
English: May I interrupt you for just a moment, Mr. Prosecutor?

Japanese: あ。ちょっと、おじょうさん。 ここはその、私がカツンと。
English: Ah, don't you worry, my dear. I have this situation well in hand.
*sniffle*

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうぞ。
English: Err, that is I... Um... G-G-Go right ahead.

Japanese: このわたしのせいで ノンちゃん、お死にになった、と？
English: Madame Fey, are you seriously accusing me of killing my sweet Dougie?

Japanese: そうよ。
English: Yes. I am.

Japanese: あなたは、呑田　菊三さんを 殺害したばかりか‥‥、
English: Not only am I saying you murdered Doug Swallow...

Japanese: その罪を、恋人である被告人・ 成歩堂　龍一に着せようとした！
English: ...but you also tried to pin the whole thing on your current lover, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: だから、そんな 言いがかりは‥‥
English: I told you that you should let me handle this...
*weep*

Japanese: あ。スミマセン。 ど、どうぞ。
English: Urk. Sorry. P-Please go ahead.

Japanese: わたし、リュウちゃんのコト お慕い申しあげておりますのよ？
English: How can you say that!? I'm absolutely devoted to my dear Feenie.

Japanese: そのリュウちゃんに 罪をお着せするなんて‥‥
English: The notion that I would try to frame him is ludicrous!

Japanese: ちなみには、 お荷物が重たすぎますわっ！
English: This is all just too much for poor little me to bear!!

Japanese: 平たく言えば“動機はなんだ、 コノヤロー”というコトぢゃの。
English: Ah, I believe the girl is trying to ask what on Earth her motive would be.

Japanese: （そのコタエは‥‥この資料の中に かくされているはず‥‥）
English: (The answer to that lies somewhere in this police report. It must!)

Japanese: 半年前、この裁判所の地下食堂で 起こった事件‥‥
English: Eight months ago, an incident occurred in the basement cafeteria of this building.

Japanese: そして‥‥事件と同じ日に 出会った、あるカップル‥‥
English: And then... that same day the two of them "accidentally" meet...

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側は‥‥美柳　ちなみさんに 証言を要求します！
English: The defense requests further testimony from Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: しょ、証言‥‥？ 何について、ですかな？
English: F-Further testimony...? What about?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥ 被告人との出会い、について。
English: About the events of the day when she first met the defendant, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: そんなノロケ話など、 事件には関係ありませんぞ！
English: What could that possibly have to do with this case!?

Japanese: “被告人に罪を着せる理由はない” ‥‥証人は、そう言いました。
English: The witness claims that she has no reason to frame the defendant, am I correct?

Japanese: 弁護側には、そのコトバに対して 反証するケンリがあります！
English: Well I have evidence that suggests that she, in fact, had a very good reason!

Japanese: いいでしょう。 弁護側の要求を認めます。
English: Very well then, the court grants the defense's request.

Japanese: ‥‥おじょうさん。 証言をおねがいできますかな？
English: Young lady. Would you mind staying on for just a bit longer?

Japanese: も、モチロンですわ‥‥
English: Of course not, Mr. Judge.

Japanese: （‥‥これからが勝負、ね。 美柳　ちなみさん‥‥）
English: (Get ready for the battle of your life, Dahlia Hawthorne!)

Japanese: ～成歩堂　龍一について～
English: -- How I Met My Feenie --

Japanese: リュウちゃんと出会ったのは、 今から半年前のことでしたわ。
English: I first met my darling Feenie eight months ago.

Japanese: 運命のスポットは‥‥この裁判所の 地下にある、資料室だったんです。
English: It's like we were destined to meet in this very courthouse's basement reading room.

Japanese: ひとめ見て‥‥なんだか、 キュンって来ちゃいましたの。
English: The moment our eyes met, my heart skipped a beat!

Japanese: それから、ずっと‥‥おつきあい させていただいております。
English: We've been going out ever since that fateful day.

Japanese: （‥‥ホント、はたメイワクなほど アツアツなんです、ぼくたち！）
English: We're so lovey-wuvey we literally make people sick! It's just jealousy, I think...

Japanese: だから、被告人！ ヒトの証言に口をはさまない！
English: Mr. Wright! Do that again and you will be held in contempt of court!

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥ これで舞台はととのったわ！）
English: (And now we enter the final act of our little drama!)

Japanese: あとは‥‥チミしだいぢゃ、 千尋クン！
English: As we used to say in the days of my youth, "Go get her!"

Japanese: ～成歩堂　龍一について～
English: -- How I Met My Feenie --

Japanese: リュウちゃんと出会ったのは、 今から半年前のことでしたわ。
English: I first met my darling Feenie eight months ago.

Japanese: 運命のスポットは‥‥この裁判所の 地下にある、資料室だったんです。
English: It's like we were destined to meet in this very courthouse's basement reading room.

Japanese: ひとめ見て‥‥なんだか、 キュンって来ちゃいましたの。
English: The moment our eyes met, my heart skipped a beat!

Japanese: それから、ずっと‥‥おつきあい させていただいております。
English: We've been going out ever since that fateful day.

Japanese: いいな、千尋クン。‥‥彼女は 裁判長のお気に入りぢゃ。
English: Listen to me, Mia. That woman has the judge in the palm of her hand, you see.

Japanese: 攻撃するためには‥‥証言に ムジュンを見つけるしかないぞ！
English: So the only way to discredit her is to find a contradiction in her testimony!

Japanese: わたし‥‥大学のレポートの資料を 探すため、裁判所に来てましたの。
English: I had come to this courthouse to do some research for a paper I was writing.

Japanese: それまでは、被害者の呑田さんと つきあっていたのですね？
English: So until that time, you had been dating Doug Swallow?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥ちなみって、 イケナイ子ですのね。
English: Y-Yes. I'm a real fool, I know.

Japanese: カンタンに心変わり してしまうなんて‥‥。
English: Letting my emotions change so quickly... I'm ashamed of myself.

Japanese: そんなコトありませんぞ！
English: No, no. Not at all!

Japanese: 私も、判決をコロコロ変えるので 有名ですからな。
English: Look at me! I'm infamous for changing my mind!

Japanese: “うつり気で浮気な裁判長”と 呼ばれておりますぞ。ほっほっ。
English: My critics have even taken to calling me "Judge Fickle"! Ho! Ho! Ho!

Japanese: （どうしてクビに ならないのかしら‥‥）
English: (Maybe you should look for a different line of work...)

Japanese: それが‥‥なぜか、いつも 正しい判決を下すんぢゃよ。
English: Despite that, however, he always, always hands down the correct verdict.

Japanese: “フシギでブキミな裁判長”とも 呼ばれておるようぢゃな。
English: That's why some people also call him "The Great Judgini".

Japanese: 裁判所の資料室‥‥ちょっと 変わった出会いだったようですね。
English: The courthouse reading room? That's a strange place to meet the love of your life.

Japanese: そんなこと、ございませんのよ。 だって、リュウちゃんは‥‥
English: That's not true, Madame Fey. After all, Feenie was...

Japanese: 芸術学部の学生さまでありながら、 弁護士さまをお目指しに。
English: Feenie was not only an art student, but he was also planning on becoming a lawyer.

Japanese: 被告人ではなく、 あなたのことです‥‥証人。
English: I'm not talking about him. I'm talking about you, Ms. Hawthorne.

Japanese: なぜ、文学部のあなたが、 裁判所の資料室にいたのかしら？
English: What was a literature student like you doing in a courthouse reading room?

Japanese: そんなこと、今回の事件とは まったく関係ありません！
English: This line of questioning is a waste of time! It has nothing to do with our murder case!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。事件と関係ない質問は 認められませんが‥‥？
English: Ms. Fey, I'm warning you. If this has nothing to do with Mr. Swallow's case...

Japanese: （裁判長は証人の味方。攻めかたを まちがえると、イタいわね‥‥）
English: (I have to remember the judge is on Dahlia's side. I'd better tread carefully...)

Japanese: （これ以上、裁判長の心証を 悪くしちゃダメよ、千尋）
English: (Mia, if the judge gets any angrier, you'll lose the whole case!)

Japanese: （ここはひとつ、ガマンして おこうかしら‥‥）
English: (I'll just have to hold my tongue for the time being.)

Japanese: それでは、おじょうさん。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Now then, young lady, please go on with your testimony.

Japanese: 被告人、成歩堂くんと 出会ったときのことを！
English: Tell us about the time you first met Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 裁判長。事件との関連は、 これから証明できるハズです。
English: Your Honor, if you'll allow me some latitude, I think I can establish relevance.

Japanese: つづけて証言をおねがいします！
English: Please ask her to continue on with her testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。おじょうさん、 質問に答えてもらえますか。
English: Very well. Young lady, I've got a simple question for you.

Japanese: なぜ、あなたが、裁判所の 地下資料室にいたのか‥‥？
English: What were you doing downstairs in the courthouse reading room?

Japanese: ええ。よろしいですわ、 オジサマ‥‥。
English: If it pleases Your Honor, the answer is simply this.

Japanese: 裁判所の資料室‥‥ちょっと 変わった出会いだったようですね。
English: The courthouse reading room? That's a strange place to meet the love of your life.

Japanese: そんなこと、ございませんのよ。 だって、リュウちゃんは‥‥
English: That's not true, Madame Fey. After all, Feenie was...

Japanese: 芸術学部の学生さまでありながら、 弁護士さまをお目指しに。
English: Feenie was not only an art student, but he was also planning on becoming a lawyer.

Japanese: 被告人ではなく、 あなたのことです‥‥証人。
English: I'm not talking about him... I'm talking about you, Ms. Hawthorne.

Japanese: なぜ、文学部のあなたが、 裁判所の資料室にいたのかしら？
English: What was a literature student like you doing in a courthouse reading room?

Japanese: ‥‥ふふ。それはベツに ふつうのコトですのよ、おばさま。
English: Tee hee. There's nothing strange about that, ma'am.

Japanese: あの‥‥なるほどくんの、 どこにキュンっと来たのですか？
English: Hmm... So what was it about Mr. Wright that made your heart malfunction like that?

Japanese: “ただのカゼひきニイさん”‥‥ 個人的には、そんな感じですけど。
English: In my personal opinion, he just looks like a typical snotty-nosed college brat.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、半年前のリュウちゃんは とっても、お元気そうでしたわ。
English: Perhaps to a woman your age. But to me, Feenie is handsome!

Japanese: カゼひきですらなければ、 それこそ“ただのニイさん”‥‥
English: Perhaps to you, Ms. Hawthorne. But to the rest of the planet, he's a dime a dozen.

Japanese: ヒトがだれかにキュンっと来る 理由など、他人にはわかりません！
English: Love is a mysterious thing, and I object to this line of questioning!

Japanese: ジッサイ、私の奥さんを見ると、 みなさんクビをかしげますぞ！
English: If you were to look at my wife for example, you might all be shocked!

Japanese: そのとおり。 私もそのヒトリです。
English: He's telling the truth. It was truly, truly shocking.

Japanese: ‥‥はかなげな、日かげに咲いた タンポポのような、たたずまい‥‥
English: "Beautiful mushroom, growing tall in the darkness. It comes from cow dung."

Japanese: リュウちゃんの、そんなところに ヨロめいてしまったのですわ。
English: That's the poem that best describes how I feel about my Feenie.

Japanese: 呑田　菊三さん‥‥被害者とは、 何か問題はなかったのですか？
English: Were there any bad feelings between you and Mr. Swallow?

Japanese: ええ。わたしたち、とっても 仲よく、お別れしましたの。
English: No, none at all. We parted on very good terms.

Japanese: そんなワケないでしょ！
English: But that can't be!

Japanese: 私どもの調査でも、ふたりは スッパリ、別れたようです。
English: Our investigation also shows that it was a clean breakup.

Japanese: え‥‥。 そ、そうなんですか？
English: Huh... Are you... Are you sure?

Japanese: どうやら、呑田くんのほうも、 それを望んでいたようですな。
English: Yes, it seems that they both wanted it that way.

Japanese: （被害者が‥‥ 別れたがっていた‥‥？）
English: (So the victim wanted to break up with her...?)

Japanese: 呑田さん‥‥被害者も、あなたと 別れたがっていたのかしら？
English: So you're saying that Mr. Swallow also wanted to break up with you?

Japanese: さ‥‥さあ、それは‥‥
English: W-Well, y-you see...

Japanese: おじょうさんに、そんなコトを 聞くのはシツレイですぞ！
English: What a cruel thing to ask a lovely young lady like this!

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに私自身は、奥さんと 別れるなど、考えたこともない！
English: By the way, I have never ONCE considered leaving MY wife!

Japanese: 聞いていません。
English: No one cares, Mr. Payne.

Japanese: （彼女‥‥否定しない、 ということは‥‥）
English: (She didn't deny it... That must mean...)

Japanese: （どうやら呑田　菊三は 気がついていたようね）
English: (Doug Swallow must have seen through her little act.)

Japanese: （‥‥美柳　ちなみの “本性”に‥‥）
English: (He must have gotten a glimpse of her true nature.)

Japanese: （被害者が美柳　ちなみを どう思っていたか‥‥？）
English: (What did the victim think of Dahlia Hawthorne...?)

Japanese: （とりあえず、今は 必要のない情報ね‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess I don't have to think about that now...)

Japanese: レポートの資料‥‥ですか？
English: You were writing a paper? On what?

Japanese: はい。現代川柳と、犯罪の関係 について、ご本を調べたくて。
English: On the relationship between modern Senryu poetry and the criminal underworld.

Japanese: ほおほお。それはキョーミ深い 研究テーマですなあ。
English: Ho ho ho. That sounds like a fascinating research idea.

Japanese: 『もの忘れ　忘れたことも 忘れてる』‥‥のような。
English: "Am I getting old? Now I've even forgotten what I've forgotten..."

Japanese: （中年のボヤキじゃない！）
English: (Again with the mid-life crisis stuff!?)

Japanese: 千尋クン。彼女がなぜ、この 裁判所に来ていたか‥‥？
English: Mia, why did that girl really come to this courthouse? Isn't that what you wanted to know?

Japanese: よもや、忘れているワケぢゃ あるまいな？
English: And speaking of forgetting things, you haven't forgotten the police report, have you?

Japanese: ‥‥せっかくの資料ぢゃ。 ちゃんと、目を通しておくれ。
English: I went through a lot of trouble to get it, m'dear, so be sure to read it carefully.

Japanese: ちなみさん‥‥あなたは、 裁判所の資料に用はなかった。
English: Ms. Hawthorne, you weren't here because of your research paper, were you?

Japanese: 他に、もっと重大な用事が あったんじゃないかしら‥‥？
English: Didn't you actually come here for a much more important reason?

Japanese: なな、なんですか！ その、イミありげなニコニコ顔は！
English: Wh-What is the meaning of that cocky smile on your face, Ms. Fey!?

Japanese: 半年前‥‥。この裁判所で、 ある事件がありました。
English: Eight months ago, right here in this very courthouse, there was another tragedy.

Japanese: じけん‥‥？
English: Another tragedy...?

Japanese: “弁護士が毒を盛られた” という、アレですかな？
English: Do you mean the incident in which an attorney was poisoned?

Japanese: この資料に、その事件の 容疑者の名前が記されています。
English: The name of the suspect in that incident is listed here in this report.

Japanese: ‥‥“美柳　ちなみ”‥‥と。
English: And that name is... Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: な、なんですと‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: み‥‥みやなぎ　ちなみ‥‥！
English: D-Dahlia H-Hawthorne!?

Japanese: そう、みなさんの大好きな 美柳　ちなみさんです！
English: Yes! The sweetie-pie of everyone's eye, Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: 彼女は、半年前の事件の 最有力容疑者だったのです！
English: She was the prime suspect in a criminal case just eight months ago!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥し、信じられません。
English: Order! Order! Order! Th-This is unbelievable!

Japanese: このように、セイソでカレンな きんぽうげの花びらのような‥‥
English: It's true then... The loveliest rose can hide the cruelest thorn...

Japanese: 弁護人！　ひ‥‥ヒキョウですぞ！
English: Ms. Fey! Th-That's not fair!

Japanese: 本件と無関係な中傷で、 この証人の信用をなくそうなどと
English: You can't slander my witness with an unrelated case!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ‥‥そのようなコトは、 この亜内　武文が！　ユルしませ
English: I, Winston Payne, will not allow it!

Japanese: 検事さん。‥‥わたしが お話ししてるんですのよ。
English: Mr. Prosecutor... I believe I was speaking.

Japanese: あ。スミマセン。 ど、どうぞ。
English: Urk. P-Pardon me... G-Go right ahead...

Japanese: 今から、半年前。 警察のみなさまは、たしかに‥‥
English: It's true that about eight months ago...

Japanese: わたしに対して、ウタガイの おマナコでいらっしゃいました。
English: ...the police expressed some interest in me.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。 ウタガイのおマナコ‥‥
English: Hmm... Expressed some interest, huh?

Japanese: わたし‥‥この場で、 証言したいのですけど。
English: Mr. Judge, sir... I know I'm under oath, so I'll tell you the absolute truth.

Japanese: 半年前の事件について、 わたしが無関係である、と‥‥
English: I did not commit the crime that occurred during that incident eight months ago.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: （さあ‥‥これで２つの事件は つながったわ！）
English: (OK... I've tied the two crimes together!)

Japanese: （あとは‥‥ 一気に攻めるだけよ、千尋！）
English: (Now I've just got to stay on the offensive!)

Japanese: いいぞ、千尋クン！
English: Well done, Mia!

Japanese: ううう‥‥ワシのハートと シリにも、火がついたぞい！
English: Ooohhh... You've really lit a fire in my heart... And my buttocks!

Japanese: ハートと、そしてシリが‥‥ 今。ウズきだしたワイっ！
English: I can hardly tell which is more inflamed, my spirit... or my hemorrhoids!

Japanese: ～半年前の事件について～
English: -- The Poisoning --

Japanese: 弁護士さんとカフェテリアで お会いして、お話ししました。
English: I met the lawyer who was poisoned to discuss something in the cafeteria that day.

Japanese: わたし、少し席をはずしました。 事件はそのとき、起こりましたの！
English: I left my seat for just a moment, and that's when it happened!

Japanese: 毒薬は液体で、致死量の１０ミリ リットルが検出されたそうです。
English: From what I heard, it was a liquid poison that is lethal at just 2 teaspoons.

Japanese: しかも、犯行に使用された毒薬は かなり特殊なモノだったそうです。
English: Not only that, I heard it was a very special kind of poison.

Japanese: わたしは無実ですわ。そんな毒薬、 手に入れる方法がありませんもの！
English: So you see, I'm innocent! I wouldn't even know where to get a poison like that!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。半年前、 そんなことがあったのですか！
English: Hmm... So that's what happened here eight months ago.

Japanese: し‥‥しかし、お聞きのとおり！ この証人は、事件とは無関係です。
English: However, as you've heard from the witness's testimony, she had nothing to do with it.

Japanese: 弁護人のヒレツなたくらみは これで‥‥
English: I think the defense is just about out of tricks...

Japanese: ザンネンね‥‥亜内検事さん。
English: I'm terribly sorry, Mr. Payne.

Japanese: 弁護側のヒレツなたくらみは まだまだ、つづきます。
English: But I'm afraid the defense has many more tricks up its sleeve today.

Japanese: ‥‥尋問が終わるまで！
English: And I'll be sure to show them to you before the end of this cross-examination!

Japanese: うぐはッ！
English: Unnggh!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんだか急に、 弁護人に妙なハクリョクが‥‥
English: What the...!? Why does the defense suddenly feel stronger...?

Japanese: おおお！　千尋クンに、 弁護士のオーラがッ！
English: Aha! Mia! You're glowing with a true lawyer's aura, m'dear!

Japanese: ちょっぴり、背が伸びた ようにみえるぞい！
English: That proud posture and self-confidence! Absolutely smashing!

Japanese: ～半年前の事件について～
English: -- The Poisoning --

Japanese: 弁護士さんとカフェテリアで お会いして、お話ししました。
English: I met the lawyer who was poisoned to discuss something in the cafeteria that day.

Japanese: わたし、少し席をはずしました。 事件はそのとき、起こりましたの！
English: I left my seat for just a moment, and that's when it happened!

Japanese: 毒薬は液体で、致死量の１０ミリ リットルが検出されたそうです。
English: From what I heard, it was a liquid poison that is lethal at just 2 teaspoons.

Japanese: しかも、犯行に使用された毒薬は かなり特殊なモノだったそうです。
English: Not only that, I heard it was a very special kind of poison.

Japanese: わたしは無実ですわ。そんな毒薬、 手に入れる方法がありませんもの！
English: So you see, I'm innocent! I wouldn't even know where to get a poison like that!

Japanese: いいかの。彼女が今まで タイホされなかった理由は‥‥
English: So in essence, the main reason Ms. Hawthorne was never arrested for this crime...

Japanese: “毒薬を入手する 手段がなかったから”ぢゃ。
English: ...was because no one could show how she could have obtained the poison.

Japanese: じゃあ、逆に言えば‥‥ その方法さえ立証すればいい！
English: Then all we have to do is find a way to establish how she could've gotten some, right?

Japanese: （‥‥彼女は、どうやって 毒薬を入手したか‥‥？）
English: (Great... Now just how did a lit. student get a hold of poison, of all things...?)

Japanese: 被害者の弁護士さんと、 どんな話をしたのかしら？
English: What were you talking about with the defense attorney?

Japanese: それは‥‥申しあげたく ありませんわ。
English: Well I... I'm sorry, but that's confidential.

Japanese: 資料によると、“ある事件のため” ‥‥ということですな。
English: According to the report, you were being interviewed regarding another case.

Japanese: 私がおさないころ、巻きこまれた 事件について話を聞きたい‥‥
English: The lawyer that was killed...

Japanese: 亡くなった弁護士さんが、 そうおっしゃったんです。
English: He said he wanted to talk about an incident I was caught up in when I was younger.

Japanese: ちなみに、それは どんな事件だったのですか？
English: Why don't you tell us all what that incident was?

Japanese: そんなコト、審理とは マッタク関係ありませんぞ！
English: That has absolutely nothing at all to do with this case!

Japanese: 検察側の異議を認めます。 ‥‥弁護側の質問は、却下。
English: Objection sustained. The defense's question is stricken from the record.

Japanese: 今回のコトといい‥‥ よく事件に巻きこまれるのね。
English: You get involved in a lot of "incidents", don't you, Ms. Hawthorne?

Japanese: ‥‥わたし、不幸のお星さまの 下に生まれてしまったのですね。
English: Well, I guess I was just born under a bad sign.

Japanese: （シンパイいらないさ！ ボクが守ってあげるから！）
English: Don't worry, Dollie! I'll protect you!

Japanese: ‥‥だ、そうです。 よかったですな、おじょうさん。
English: You heard the man. Now THAT is true love, young lady!

Japanese: もお‥‥。リュウちゃんたら‥‥。
English: Oh, Feenie... Please...

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ね）
English: (*gag* Those two really are making me ill. Albeit for decidedly different reasons.)

Japanese: どれぐらいの時間、 席をはずしたのですか？
English: How long were you gone?

Japanese: それは、警察のみなさまにも お話しいたしましたけど‥‥、
English: I've already answered all these questions for the police...

Japanese: １０分か、２０分‥‥だったと 思います。
English: But if you must know, maybe 10 or 20 minutes...

Japanese: その時間、あなたはどこへ？ ‥‥トイレかしら？
English: And where were you during that stretch of time? Using the toilet?

Japanese: （ナニ言ってるんだよ 千尋さん！）
English: What are you SAYING, Miss Fey!?

Japanese: （ちいちゃんは、トイレなんか 行かないんだい！）
English: Toilet!? My perfect little Dollie doesn't poop!

Japanese: ‥‥だ、そうです。 ザンネンでしたな、弁護人。
English: You heard the defendant, Ms. Fey. Better luck next time.

Japanese: もお‥‥。リュウちゃんたら‥‥。
English: Oh, Feenie... Please...

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、それはすでに 警察が調べているでしょう。
English: The police have already looked into this whole matter.

Japanese: この審理に関係ない以上、 イミのない質問です！
English: This line of questioning is nothing but a waste of the court's time!

Japanese: 異議を認めます。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Objection sustained. Ms. Hawthorne, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: １０ミリリットルというのは、 どれぐらいの量なんでしょう？
English: About how much liquid is 2 teaspoons?

Japanese: ふむ、そうですなあ。
English: Hmm, well let me see...

Japanese: 私の目薬の小ビンが、ええと‥‥ １５ミリリットル入りですな。
English: My bottle of eyedrops says it's 1/2 fluid ounce... Which is equal to 3 teaspoons...

Japanese: （その３分の２、か。 ちょっとした量ね‥‥）
English: (So it's about two-thirds of that amount, huh...)

Japanese: 弁護士さんが飲み残したコーヒーの カップに、毒が入っていたんです。
English: The poison was found in the lawyer's mug of coffee.

Japanese: きっと、わたしが 席をはずしたあとに‥‥、
English: It must have been after I left the table...

Japanese: どなたかが、そっと 毒をお入れになったのですわ。
English: Someone must have quietly slipped it in there.

Japanese: “特殊”‥‥と言うと？
English: A "special kind of poison"? How so?

Japanese: なんでも、すごく検出が むずかしい猛毒だったそうです。
English: Well, I heard that it's almost impossible to detect.

Japanese: ほほお‥‥。 それはまた、どうして‥‥？
English: Oh? And where would something like that come from...?

Japanese: 詳しくは存じませんの。 おまわりさまのお話では、
English: I'm sorry... All I know is what I overheard the policemen saying.

Japanese: なんでも、高度な化学的処理で 精製されたもの、だとか‥‥。
English: They said something about using advanced chemical processes to purify it...

Japanese: かがくてきしょり‥‥ですか。 それはその。なんというかあの。
English: "Chemical processes"...? Well, well... That's quite...

Japanese: ‥‥スゴそうですなあ。
English: ...impressive. Most impressive...

Japanese: （‥‥そんなもの、犯人は どこで手に入れたのかしら‥‥）
English: (The better question is, how did the criminal get something like that?)

Japanese: それが‥‥逮捕されずに すんだ理由ですか。
English: And that's the reason they didn't arrest you?

Japanese: “毒薬を手に入れる方法が なかったから”‥‥
English: Because no one could show how you could've gotten the poison?

Japanese: あの。‥‥じゅうぶんだと 思いますけど。
English: I think that's a good enough reason, Madame Fey.

Japanese: さよう。くだらない尋問は これぐらいにしていただきたい！
English: She's right. And I think we've all had enough of Ms. Fey's questions!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: “毒薬を入手する方法はなかった” そんなイイワケは通用しないわ。
English: You wouldn't know how to get that kind of poison? I don't believe you.
What...!?

Japanese: あなたは、いつでも毒薬を 手に入れることができたハズです。
English: In fact, you had easy access to that kind of poison, didn't you?

Japanese: ‥‥“彼”の研究室に 行きさえすれば。
English: At your boyfriend's lab!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ 被害者の呑田　菊三くん！
English: B-Boyfriend!? You mean the victim, Doug Swallow!?

Japanese: 半年前‥‥。彼女は 呑田　菊三さんとつきあっていた。
English: That's right. Up until eight months ago, Ms. Hawthorne was dating Mr. Swallow.

Japanese: 彼は‥‥勇盟大学、 薬学部の学生です。
English: And if you'll recall, Mr. Swallow was a Pharmacology student at Ivy University.

Japanese: ‥‥やくがく‥‥ぶ‥‥
English: Ph-Ph-Pharmacology...

Japanese: 研究室には、化学実験に使用する 最新の設備もあります。
English: His laboratory contained highly advanced chemistry equipment.

Japanese: 逆に言えば‥‥それしか あり得ないのではないでしょうか？
English: In fact, without such equipment...

Japanese: ‥‥犯人が“特殊な毒薬”を 手に入れたルートは！
English: ...the culprit could never have obtained such a rare and special poison!

Japanese: いかがかしら、ちなみさん？ 毒薬を手に入れる方法は、あった。
English: Well, Ms. Hawthorne? It seems you had access to such a poison after all.

Japanese: ‥‥あとは、被害者のスキを見て、 カップにその毒を入れるだけ‥‥
English: And then, it was a matter of slipping it into the victim's coffee when he wasn't looking.

Japanese: それができたのは、いっしょの テーブルにいた、あなただけよ！
English: The only person who could've done that was the one sitting at his very table -- you!

Japanese: ‥‥いやあああああああッ！
English: NOOOOOOOOOOOO!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ま‥‥まさか！　まさか‥‥
English: Order! Order! Order! C-C-Could it be...!?

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥まったく コンキョのない話でありま
English: Th-That's nothing but a baseless accus--

Japanese: ひとつだけ、いいですか？ ‥‥おばさま。
English: May I say something, Madame Fey?

Japanese: 何かしら？　‥‥ちなみさん。
English: What is it, Ms. Hawthorne?

Japanese: おコーヒーに入れられた毒は、 １０ミリリットルですよね。
English: The amount of poison in the coffee was 2 teaspoons, correct?

Japanese: それだけの量の液体を運ぶには、 いれもの‥‥容器が必要ですわ。
English: In order to carry that much liquid... you would need some kind of container.

Japanese: そうですな。‥‥たしかに。
English: Well, yes... That's true.

Japanese: わたし、事件が発見されたあと、 すぐに身体検査を受けました。
English: I was searched immediately after the incident took place.

Japanese: たしか、裁判所内もテッテー的に 捜査されたと聞いていますぞ。
English: Quite true. In fact the entire courthouse was turned upside down.

Japanese: でも‥‥そんな“容器”は どこにもありませんでしたのよ？
English: But... they didn't find a suspicious container anywhere, did they?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥資料にも そう書いてある‥‥！）
English: (She's right... They even mentioned that in the report!)

Japanese: そ、そんなモノ‥‥どうとでも 始末できるはずよ！
English: W-Well, you could've easily gotten rid of something that small!

Japanese: そうは行きませんわ、おばさま！
English: Excuse me, Madame, but this is a court of law!

Japanese: わたしが“おクスリのいれものを 始末した”とおっしゃるなら、
English: If you're saying I threw the poison container away...

Japanese: ‥‥お証拠品を お見せになってくださいっ！
English: ...I think you need to show some kind of proof!

Japanese: お、“お証拠品”‥‥ッ！ （は、反撃してきたわ‥‥！）
English: P-Proof...!? (She got me good with that!)

Japanese: お証拠品で立証できないかぎり、 これ以上のギロンは認めません！
English: Provide some evidence or I'll have to disallow this line of questioning, Ms. Fey!

Japanese: ここはもう、お証拠品を たたきつけるしかないぞい！
English: Unless we can come up with some evidence, we're going to lose this lead!

Japanese: （事件発生直後、毒を入れた “容器”が消えてしまった）
English: (The police conducted a full body search of Dahlia and of the entire courthouse.)

Japanese: （彼女自身は身体検査を受け、 裁判所内も捜査された‥‥）
English: (And yet the container holding the poison disappeared right after the crime occurred...)

Japanese: このおじょうさんが 犯人だと言うのならば‥‥
English: If you're going to accuse the young lady of committing the murder...

Japanese: “毒の容器”は、どこへ消えたか？ あなたの考えをうかがいましょう！
English: ...then where is the container the poison was carried in? What happened to it?
‥‥‥<Payne> ‥‥‥‥‥<Dahlia>

Japanese: あの‥‥弁護人。 わかっていますかな。
English: Ms. Fey... I'm sure even you understand.

Japanese: これはその。いわゆる 《重要な場面》なのですが‥‥？
English: In a narrative, this is what they call the "moment of truth"! And you've ruined it!

Japanese: うーんとしかって おやりになって‥‥オジサマ。
English: That's correct, Mr. Judge... But you play your part so well.

Japanese: む！　オジサマに まかせなさいッ！
English: Leave it to me, young lady! It's my job, after all!

Japanese: （く‥‥ッ！ 泣いちゃダメよ、千尋！）
English: (Ohhh...! Don't do it, Mia! Don't cry!)

Japanese: あ、あと一歩ぢゃぞ！ 千尋クン！　考えるのぢゃ！
English: You're so close, Mia! Don't give up! Think!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、大急ぎで“容器”を 始末しなければならなかった。
English: You were forced to get rid of the container in a hurry, weren't you?

Japanese: だから‥‥それを、事件と 無関係な人物に押しつけた！
English: And that's why you passed it on to someone that had nothing to do with the case!

Japanese: ‥‥身体検査をされるはずのない、 ある人物にね。
English: Someone that you knew wouldn't be searched!

Japanese: い、いったい、ダレですかッ！
English: W-Who is this person!?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一です。 ‥‥もちろん。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright, of course.

Japanese: 被告人が‥‥この証人の 共犯だった、と‥‥？
English: So the defendant was this witness's accomplice?

Japanese: とんでもありません。
English: Of course not.

Japanese: 毒薬は‥‥そう。彼女からの プレゼントだったのです。
English: She gave the poison to him disguised as... a present.

Japanese: あ‥‥！ そ、それは‥‥ッ！
English: Wh-What...!? B-But... But that's...!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ キレイなペンダントですな。
English: Hmm... That's a charming little necklace.

Japanese: これは‥‥ガラスの小ビン、 ですか。ヒジョーに愛らしい。
English: Is this... a little bottle? It's really quite cute.

Japanese: で‥‥これが どうかしましたかな？　弁護人。
English: So what about it? What does it mean, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: 証人が、成歩堂　龍一と 運命の出会いを果たしたのは‥‥
English: The day that the witness met and fell for Mr. Phoenix Wright was eight months ago...

Japanese: 半年前の、８月２７日。 ‥‥事件があった日でした。
English: August 27th. The very same day as the poisoning incident.

Japanese: そのとき彼女は、彼に これを“プレゼント”したのです。
English: Under the pretense of love, the witness gave my client a present.

Japanese: ‥‥決定的な証拠を “かくしてしまう”ために！
English: All for the purpose of hiding the one piece of evidence that would give her away!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですってええッ！ まさか、この中に‥‥モードクが！
English: Whaaat!? Are you saying there's a deadly poison in here!?

Japanese: 小ビンの中には‥‥もう、 毒薬は残っていません。
English: No, there's no longer any poison in that bottle.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: 薬学部で分析すれば‥‥ かならず痕跡が見つかるはずです！
English: I'm certain if the crime lab were to analyze it, they would find a trace amount!

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああ ああああああああああああああッ！
English: NOOOOOOOOOO!! WAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH!!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛にいいッ！ ‥‥ええと、その‥‥
English: Or-Order! Order in the court! Ahh, umm...

Japanese: ちいちゃんのかわりに 異議ありッ！
English: On behalf of Dollie, I object!

Japanese: なな、なんですか被告人ッ！
English: M-Mr. Wright! Control yourself!

Japanese: ユルさないぞお！ ち、ちいちゃんをイジめると‥‥！
English: I-I won't let you bully her like this...!

Japanese: こ、コラコラ！　だから、 勝手に出てこないように！
English: Mr. Wright! I thought I told you to stay in your seat!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Mr. Wright... Why?

Japanese: どうして‥‥そこまでして、 この証人をかばおうとするの？
English: Why are you going through so much trouble to protect her?

Japanese: え‥‥っ！
English: Why?

Japanese: だ、だって！　ぼく‥‥ 彼女に、クビったけだから！
English: B-Because...! Because I'm madly in love with her!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmmm, hmmmm...

Japanese: ひさしぶりに聞きましたな。 “クビったけ”‥‥
English: "Madly in love"... I haven't heard anyone say that in a long time...

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥あなたは、 考えたことがあるかしら。
English: Mr. Wright... Have you ever thought about this...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが、“なぜ” あなたとつきあっているか？
English: Why exactly would a woman like Dahlia Hawthorne want to date you anyway?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥モチロン、 ボクにクビったけ‥‥
English: W-Well... I guess she must be madly in love with me too...

Japanese: （なるほどくん‥‥おねがい！ もう、目をさまして‥‥）
English: (Mr. Wright... Please! Open your eyes...!)

Japanese: ‥‥審理がここまで来れば、 だれでもわかることなのよ。
English: At this point in the trial, I think it should be obvious to everyone.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが、 あなたに近づいた理由は‥‥！
English: The real reason that Dahlia Hawthorne is dating you is...

Japanese: もちろん‥‥彼女も、あなたに クビったけだったからです！
English: Naturally it's because she has fallen madly in love with you too!

Japanese: そーですよね、ヤッパリ！
English: See? Just like I told you!

Japanese: ちち、千尋クン！ ナニを言い出すんぢゃ！
English: M-M-Mia! Are you thinking clearly!?

Japanese: ‥‥今までのハナシが みんな、パアぢゃないかい！
English: Th-That flies in the face of everything we've learned up to this point!

Japanese: す、すみません！　なんだか 気に入っちゃって。
English: I-I'm sorry! I don't know why I even said that...

Japanese: “クビったけ”っていう ヒビキが‥‥
English: *sigh* I guess it's because I just like the sound of that phrase, "madly in love"...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Ms. Fey.

Japanese: いつまでも、ユメ見る少女では いられませんぞ。
English: You can't remain a starry-eyed maiden for the rest of your life, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、 考えなおします‥‥。
English: Please let me think it over one more time, Your Honor...

Japanese: もちろん‥‥あなたの 口止めをするためです！
English: Naturally, it's to keep you quiet!

Japanese: く‥‥口止めですかッ！
English: What...!? To k-keep me quiet!?

Japanese: ‥‥その。‥‥ナニをですか？
English: Um... Keep me quiet about what...?
Well... uh...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、カンジンの本人に 自覚がないようですな。
English: It seems that the rest of us don't quite understand you, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさんの“ヒミツ”を ニギっているという自覚が‥‥
English: Nor do we understand what this vile secret that Ms. Hawthorne holds is...

Japanese: そうですよ、千尋さん。
English: That's because there is no secret, I tell you.

Japanese: （ああ‥‥なんて ノンキなの、あなたは！）
English: (ARGH!! How dense can you be!?)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。もう一度 考えなおしてもらいましょうか。
English: Ms. Fey... Please think it over and try again.

Japanese: はい‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは‥‥あなたに クビったけだったワケじゃない。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne was not and is not madly in love with you.

Japanese: ねらいは‥‥クビに下がっていた ペンダントだけだったのよ。
English: The only thing she's after is that bottle necklace you love to wear around your neck!

Japanese: ぺんだんと‥‥？
English: My n-necklace...?

Japanese: さっき、控え室で あなたは言っていたわ。
English: Back there in the waiting room, you said it yourself...

Japanese: 彼女、テレ屋さんだから‥‥ 会うたびに、ボクに言うんですよ。
English: Yeah, but she's so shy. Every time I see her, she always says the same thing to me.

Japanese: “やっぱりそれ、返して”って。
English: "Please give it back now."

Japanese: ヘンな女ぢゃの。一度あげたモノを 返せ、とは‥‥
English: What a strange girl, asking for a present back like that...

Japanese: このペンダントは、彼女にとって 致命的な証拠品だった。
English: For Dahlia Hawthorne, that necklace is irrefutable evidence of her crime.

Japanese: だから‥‥なんとしても、 彼女は回収したかったのよ。
English: That's why she absolutely had to get it back.

Japanese: う、ウソだ‥‥！
English: Y-You're lying!

Japanese: でも、あなたは返さなかった。
English: But you never gave it back to her.

Japanese: その上、会うヒトに、いちいち うれしそうに見せびらかしていた。
English: And to make things worse for her, you insisted on showing it to everyone you met.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、彼女は‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: That's why she......

Japanese: ‥‥そんなの‥‥そんなの‥‥
English: ...I don't...... I don't believe you...

Japanese: ウソだわわああああああああんっ！
English: NOOO!! Th-That's a LIE!! Waaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Eeeeeek!!

Japanese: （ち、千尋クン！ しっかりするんぢゃアッ！）
English: M-Mia! Are you alright!?

Japanese: （ひ、被告人が‥‥ 逃げ出したぞおッ！）
English: Ah! The defendant! He-He's getting away!

Japanese: （係官ッ！　すぐ‥‥ すぐ、追うのです！　早くッ！）
English: Bailiff! Hurry! After him!

Japanese: ‥‥大丈夫かッ！　千尋クン！
English: Mia! Mia! Are you alright!?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。‥‥なんとか。
English: Y-Yes, I-I think so...

Japanese: あのボウヤ‥‥ トンデモないのお、まったく！
English: That boy...! He went completely insane!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 なるほどくんは‥‥？
English: Where... Where's Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 係官たちが捕まえたそうぢゃ。 じき、戻ってくるぢゃろ。
English: It looks like the bailiff caught him, so he should be brought back here soon.

Japanese: よかった‥‥。 ‥‥‥‥‥‥ああああっ！
English: Thank goodness... Oh no!

Japanese: ここ、今度はなんぢゃ？
English: What is it!?

Japanese: しょ、証拠品が‥‥あの、 ペンダントが‥‥
English: The bottle necklace...! Ms. Hawthorne's "present"...!

Japanese: なくなっていますっ！
English: It's gone...!

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥、ナニぃぃぃぃッ！
English: Whaaaaat...! That's terrible!!

Japanese: さっき、ぶつかってきたとき ‥‥取られちゃいました！
English: Mr. Wright must have grabbed it when he slammed into me!

Japanese: バカな‥‥バカなボウヤぢゃ。 たった１つの証拠品を‥‥
English: Foolish boy...! That's the only thing that could have saved him...

Japanese: どうするつもりぢゃあああァァッ！
English: What in blazes are we supposed to do noooow!!

Japanese: ‥‥被告人。こんなコトは、 まさに前代未聞です。
English: Mr. Wright!! This sort of behavior is unprecedented in the history of this court!

Japanese: ゴメンなさい。
English: I-I'm sorry...

Japanese: ゴメンですめば、ここにいる 私たち全員、クビですぞッ！
English: I'm afraid that your apology is not enough!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 証拠品を‥‥ ペンダントを、どうしたの？
English: What did you do with the bottle necklace?

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴメン、千尋さん。 ぼく‥‥ぼく‥‥
English: F-Forgive me... I... I... I'm sorry...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいわ。 ペンダントを返してほしいの。
English: It's OK. Just give back the necklace.

Japanese: 食べちゃいました。
English: I ate it.
You what?

Japanese: た‥‥た。たべた‥‥？
English: You... You... You ate it...?

Japanese: ‥‥ガラス製でカタかったけど。 バリバリ噛みくだいて。
English: It was too big to swallow, so I had to chew it into little bits first, but yeah...

Japanese: ‥‥う。 うえええええええええええええん！
English: ...Ugh. Aaaaaaaaahhhh!
What the...?

Japanese: なんという、バカな‥‥ッ！
English: What is he doing now!?

Japanese: さ、裁判長ッ！　すぐに閉廷を！
English: Y-Your Honor!! You've got to stop the trial!!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　なるほどくん！ おなか、イタくない？
English: Mr. Wright! Mr. Wright! Are you feeling OK!? Does your stomach hurt!?

Japanese: あなたが食べたペンダントには、 猛毒が‥‥！
English: That bottle you swallowed may have had some poison left in it!

Japanese: ふ‥‥うふふふふふふふふふ‥‥
English: Ehee hee... Hee, hee, hee, hee, hee...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥身をもって、 証明してくれたようですなあ。
English: It seems the defendant has proven the prosecution's case for us.

Japanese: あのペンダントには、 毒など入っていなかった‥‥と。
English: Clearly that bottle did not contain a deadly poison!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: H-How can you be so sure!?

Japanese: ジジツ‥‥、
English: Hee, hee, hee... I think that's obvious...

Japanese: ‥‥こうして被告人は おめおめと生きている。
English: As you can see, the defendant is still very much alive.

Japanese: 毒‥‥？　どうやら、新米弁護士の 妄想だったようですなあ‥‥
English: As for the poison? More like a fledgling defense attorney's overactive imagination!

Japanese: ふ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm... So it would seem.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなハズ、ありません！
English: No! There must be some mistake!

Japanese: あのペンダントには、もう毒液は 入っていなかった！
English: The bottle must not have had any poison left in it!

Japanese: 薬品の毒性が、すでに 消えてしまったと考えれば‥‥
English: Either that or the poison must have lost its potency...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。いいですかな、 新米弁護士さん。
English: There, there... It's alright, rookie.

Japanese: “被告人を信頼する”‥‥ それは、タイヘンよいことです。
English: Trusting your client is the most noble thing a defense attorney can do.

Japanese: あなたはこれまで、つねに 成歩堂くんを信頼してきた。
English: And it's heartwarming to see that you placed this much faith in Mr. Wright.

Japanese: しかし！　それは、ワレワレも 同じコトなのです！
English: But that's how it is for us on the prosecution side, too!

Japanese: 私は‥‥証人・美柳　ちなみさんを イノチをかけて信頼している！
English: For example, I would trust the witness, Ms. Hawthorne, with my very life!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたのスイリは、 まちがっていたのですよ！
English: Which is why I can state that your assessment of her is completely wrong!

Japanese: そこまでッ！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥弁護人。 あなたの主張は、認められません。
English: Unfortunately, Ms. Fey, I cannot accept your explanation of the events.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか‥‥！
English: B-But why!?

Japanese: 新米のあなたには わからないかもしれませんが‥‥
English: This may be impossible for a beginner like you to understand...

Japanese: 法廷では、証拠品が すべてだからです。
English: ...but in a court of law, evidence is everything.

Japanese: うう‥‥ッ！
English: Unngggh!

Japanese: （こ、ここまできて‥‥ 逃げられてしまうの‥‥？）
English: (Even though I've proven so much, is she going to get away with everything...?)

Japanese: それでは、美柳　ちなみさんの 容疑も晴れたことですので、
English: Well, now that the suspicion surrounding Ms. Hawthorne has been cleared up...

Japanese: 審理をつづけたいと‥‥
English: I would like to proceed with the trial.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ゴメン、千尋さん。 ボク、忘れてました。
English: I'm sorry, Miss Fey. It totally slipped my mind.

Japanese: ‥‥こんなボクのコト‥‥
English: I'm really, really sorry...

Japanese: 千尋さんは、ホントに 信じてくれていたんですよね。
English: I know you believed in me, and I feel like I really let you down.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥
English: Mr. Wright... What are you trying to say?

Japanese: じつは、ぼく‥‥ 言ってなかったことがあるんです。
English: Um... There's something I forgot to tell you.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: What is it!?

Japanese: あの日‥‥アイツ‥‥ 呑田　菊三と会ったとき‥‥
English: That day... The day I met Doug Swallow...

Japanese: ‥‥彼女とは、 もう会わないほうがいい。
English: That girl... You shouldn't see her anymore.

Japanese: そんなコト、 きみに言われたくないよ！
English: Hey! It's none of your business!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタのためなんだ。 きっと、よくないことが起こる。
English: I'm telling you for your sake. If you continue to see her, it's going to be bad news.

Japanese: う、ウソだッ！
English: Y-You're lying!

Japanese: いいか、聞くんだ。 ‥‥あの女はなぁ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
